epic_name: MVP PRD — Mobile Quarterly Export in 10 Minutes (UK Sole Traders, Bank-Feed First)
summary: Decomposed the MVP into granular backend, data, export, evidence, voice, UI, and validation tasks with conservative dependencies and explicit UI viewability requirements for all user-facing screens.
warnings: The epic does not specify the mobile/backend stack, Open Banking provider, OCR vendor, or PDF generation library; tasks infer implementation-neutral contracts and services first.
UI tasks assume a locally runnable preview environment with a localhost URL, even if the final delivery targets mobile devices, to satisfy viewability and review requirements.
Duplicate detection tolerances, merchant fuzzy-match thresholds, and export formatting details are under-specified and should be finalized during implementation without expanding MVP scope.
HMRC submission, VAT, multi-business, payroll, and complex tax adjustments remain explicitly out of scope and should not leak into task execution.

tasks.title: Define canonical finance domain schemas and MVP category taxonomy
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical schemas, enums, and auditable field contracts used across ingestion, triage, readiness, evidence, and export.
tasks.purpose: Create the shared source of truth for transactions, classifications, evidence, quarters, rules, and export records so downstream implementation is consistent and reviewable.
tasks.input: Epic requirements for export fields, blocking rules, category set, audit requirements, and minimum entities.
tasks.output: Versioned schema/spec artifacts for BankTransaction, TransactionClassification, Evidence, EvidenceLink, Quarter, QuarterMetrics, Rule, export rows, blocker enums, and category enums.
tasks.action: Author the canonical schema definitions, stable category/enum lists, field constraints, nullable rules, and audit-field requirements; document which fields drive blocking and export readiness.
verification: Schema definitions cover every entity and export field named in the epic.
Blocking-rule fields map cleanly to RESOLVED vs BLOCKING EXPORT logic with no ambiguity.
Category codes exactly match the MVP list and distinguish categories from tags.
Downstream consumers for ingestion, UI, voice, matching, and export are identified in the schema notes.

tasks.title: Implement persistence models and migrations for core entities
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical schemas, enums, and auditable field contracts used across ingestion, triage, readiness, evidence, and export.
tasks.purpose: Translate the canonical domain contracts into durable application models and database migrations that support idempotent import, audit history, quarter metrics, and rule storage.
tasks.input: A1 schemas and current repository persistence conventions.
tasks.output: Database migrations, ORM/data-access models, indexes, uniqueness constraints, and seed/reference data for categories and blocker enums.
tasks.action: Create storage models and migrations, including uniqueness/index strategy for imported bank transactions, audit history persistence, and reference tables or enum mappings required by the MVP.
verification: Migrations apply cleanly on a fresh database and on a staged upgrade path.
Uniqueness constraints prevent duplicate transaction imports on refresh.
Audit fields persist old/new values for user edits on classification-related entities.
Reference data for MVP categories is seeded or otherwise available to application code.

tasks.title: Add secure token storage, encryption baselines, and legal disclaimer surfaces
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the canonical schemas, enums, and auditable field contracts used across ingestion, triage, readiness, evidence, and export.
tasks.purpose: Cover the MVP non-functional requirements for credential handling, encryption, and visible user disclaimers before connecting external finance data.
tasks.input: Epic non-functional requirements and the application's existing configuration/secrets patterns.
tasks.output: Secure secret-storage integration, encryption configuration, token-handling service boundaries, and reusable disclaimer content/component hooks.
tasks.action: Implement secure token persistence boundaries, document encryption expectations, and add reusable disclaimer support so UI and export surfaces can display the required warning consistently.
verification: Provider credentials are stored via approved secret-handling mechanisms and not hard-coded in source.
Transport and at-rest encryption expectations are documented in implementation artifacts.
A reusable disclaimer string/component is available for onboarding, quarter export, or equivalent user-facing surfaces.
Failure paths avoid leaking credentials or raw provider secrets into logs.

tasks.title: Implement Open Banking connection flow and account import bootstrap
tasks.workstream_goal: Connect read-only bank feeds and deliver reliable, duplicate-safe transaction ingestion for the MVP inbox.
tasks.purpose: Enable first-time setup to connect a bank account through a read-only Open Banking provider and bootstrap the initial 90-day transaction import.
tasks.input: A2 persistence models, A3 secure token storage, and provider selection/configuration.
tasks.output: Provider integration service, bank-connection session flow, linked BankAccount records, and initial import bootstrap job or command.
tasks.action: Implement the provider auth/consent exchange, map linked accounts into local records, and trigger the initial transaction import window needed for onboarding.
verification: A successful connect flow creates a linked bank account record and stores provider tokens securely.
Initial import requests approximately the last 90 days of transactions as specified by the epic.
Read-only permissions are enforced and documented.
Connection failures return actionable user-safe errors without exposing provider secrets.

tasks.title: Build idempotent transaction ingestion and refresh pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Connect read-only bank feeds and deliver reliable, duplicate-safe transaction ingestion for the MVP inbox.
tasks.purpose: Ingest and refresh bank transactions repeatedly without creating duplicates, preserving stable references for triage, matching, and export.
tasks.input: A1 schemas, A2 persistence, and B1 provider connectivity.
tasks.output: Import pipeline, deduplication logic, refresh scheduler/manual refresh entrypoint, normalized BankTransaction records, and ingestion diagnostics.
tasks.action: Normalize provider transactions into canonical fields, enforce idempotency rules, and add refresh support so inbox and quarter calculations operate on current data.
verification: Re-importing the same provider payload does not create duplicate BankTransaction rows.
Imported records preserve stable transaction references needed for export traceability.
Refresh updates new or changed transactions without corrupting prior classifications.
Ingestion diagnostics identify imported, skipped, and duplicate-suppressed counts.

tasks.title: Create inbox transaction query layer with blocker preflags and performance guardrails
tasks.workstream_goal: Connect read-only bank feeds and deliver reliable, duplicate-safe transaction ingestion for the MVP inbox.
tasks.purpose: Prepare the inbox-facing data retrieval path so user triage sees only relevant transactions, existing classifications, and blocker reasons within the performance budget.
tasks.input: A1 contracts, A2 persistence, and B2 imported transaction data.
tasks.output: Inbox query/service layer, transaction DTOs with blocker flags, sorting/filtering behavior, and performance tests or benchmarks.
tasks.action: Implement the read model powering inbox and queue screens, including blocker status projection, transaction ordering, and indexes/query plans required for typical sub-2-second loads.
verification: Inbox read models expose blocker reasons needed by the triage and finish-now flows.
Typical-device or representative dataset tests demonstrate inbox load below the 2 second target.
Sorting/filtering preserve deterministic ordering for unresolved work.
Query paths handle transactions with or without evidence links.

tasks.title: Implement classification suggestion and micro-decision command handlers
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved quarter-ready records through fast classification, split, and review actions.
tasks.purpose: Provide the core action layer for category assignment, business/personal tagging, split percentages, and audit logging of each user decision.
tasks.input: A1 schema rules, A2 persistence, B2 transactions, and B3 inbox read model needs.
tasks.output: Classification service, suggestion API/logic, mutation handlers for category/business-personal/split updates, and audit trail records.
tasks.action: Implement write operations and optional suggestions for transaction classification so users and later voice flows can apply micro-decisions consistently and reversibly.
verification: Category, business/personal, and split actions persist correctly and update audit history.
Split validation enforces required percentage values when split mode is enabled.
Classification confidence is stored where applicable without overwriting explicit user decisions incorrectly.
Updated classifications are visible immediately through inbox and quarter read models.

tasks.title: Implement duplicate detection and resolution workflow
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved quarter-ready records through fast classification, split, and review actions.
tasks.purpose: Handle one of the explicit export blockers by identifying potential duplicate transactions and requiring a dismiss or merge resolution path.
tasks.input: A1 blocker definitions, A2 persistence, and B2 normalized transaction data.
tasks.output: Duplicate-detection heuristics, duplicate review records/state, dismiss/merge actions, and audit entries for duplicate resolutions.
tasks.action: Detect likely duplicates using conservative heuristics, expose them as unresolved blockers, and implement explicit dismiss or merge resolution actions with traceable outcomes.
verification: Potential duplicates are surfaced with a resolution state that can block export until handled.
Dismiss and merge actions produce durable, auditable outcomes.
Duplicate logic avoids silently deleting original transaction records needed for traceability.
Resolved duplicate status is reflected in blocker counts and queue ordering.

tasks.title: Compute resolved vs blocking transaction state from canonical rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn imported transactions into resolved quarter-ready records through fast classification, split, and review actions.
tasks.purpose: Centralize the business logic that decides whether a transaction is export-ready or still blocking, so UI and export gating stay consistent.
tasks.input: A1 blocking definitions, C1 classification mutations, and C2 duplicate resolution states.
tasks.output: Blocking-state evaluator, reason-code mapping, readiness projections per transaction, and tests for all blocker combinations.
tasks.action: Implement a canonical evaluator that marks each transaction as resolved or blocking based strictly on the MVP rules and exposes reason codes for queue ordering and UI messaging.
verification: All four blocking conditions from the epic are implemented and covered by tests.
Evidence absence does not block export readiness.
Transactions transition from blocking to resolved immediately after required fields are set.
Readiness outputs are consumable by inbox, quarter metrics, and export enablement logic.

tasks.title: Build quarter selection and readiness metrics service
tasks.workstream_goal: Calculate quarter readiness, drive the finish-now flow, and generate the accountant-friendly quarterly pack.
tasks.purpose: Produce quarter-level totals and readiness percentages from transaction states so the user can see progress and know whether export is available.
tasks.input: B2 transaction history, C3 resolved/blocking states, and quarter-period rules.
tasks.output: Quarter service, Quarter and QuarterMetrics records, readiness calculations, and APIs/queries for total transactions, blockers, and readiness percentage.
tasks.action: Implement quarter derivation and metrics calculation across a selected period, including persistence or caching strategy for quick quarter screen loads.
verification: Readiness percentage matches the epic formula exactly.
A quarter with 200 transactions and 8 blockers can report '8 items left' and 96% readiness.
Quarter metrics update when underlying transaction blocking state changes.
Export enablement can rely on blocking_txns_count equaling zero.

tasks.title: Implement Finish Now ordered blocking queue service
tasks.workstream_goal: Calculate quarter readiness, drive the finish-now flow, and generate the accountant-friendly quarterly pack.
tasks.purpose: Turn readiness metrics into a focused, ordered exception queue that only shows export-blocking items in the prescribed resolution order.
tasks.input: D1 quarter metrics and C3 blocker reason codes.
tasks.output: Finish-now queue service/API, ordered blocker DTOs, queue counts, and transition behavior for removing resolved items.
tasks.action: Build the queue generator that returns only blocking transactions ordered as uncategorised, business/personal missing, split missing, then duplicates.
verification: Queue includes only export-blocking transactions for the selected quarter.
Ordering follows the epic-prescribed blocker sequence.
Resolving an item removes it from the queue without requiring a full reload or manual refresh trigger.
Queue count stays in sync with quarter metrics and export gating.

tasks.title: Generate Quarterly Pack CSV and PDF artifacts with traceability
tasks.workstream_goal: Calculate quarter readiness, drive the finish-now flow, and generate the accountant-friendly quarterly pack.
tasks.purpose: Produce the accountant-friendly export package only when the quarter is fully resolved, including all required CSVs and the summary PDF.
tasks.input: A1 export schemas, D1 quarter metrics, D2 blocking gate, C1 classifications, and evidence linkage data.
tasks.output: Transactions.csv, EvidenceIndex.csv, QuarterlySummary.csv, QuarterlyPack.pdf, export packaging routine, and export manifest/log.
tasks.action: Implement export assembly, totals aggregation, traceability links, and PDF summarization so a complete quarterly pack is produced when no blockers remain.
verification: Export is disabled or rejected when blocking_txns_count is greater than zero.
All four artifacts are generated with the exact required field sets, including empty EvidenceIndex.csv when no evidence exists.
Summary totals reconcile with transaction-level data and category rollups.
PDF includes period, totals, 100% readiness at export time, audit counts, and evidence coverage metrics.

tasks.title: Implement evidence capture, storage, and metadata extraction pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Make evidence optional but useful by capturing receipts and matching them to bank transactions with explicit user confirmation.
tasks.purpose: Allow users to attach receipt or document evidence, store it securely, and extract candidate matching metadata without making evidence a blocker.
tasks.input: A1 evidence schema, A2 persistence, and application file-storage conventions.
tasks.output: Evidence upload/capture service, secure storage records, extraction pipeline for amount/date/merchant, and evidence status read model.
tasks.action: Implement evidence ingestion and best-effort metadata extraction so receipts can later be matched against bank transactions while remaining non-blocking for export.
verification: Evidence can be stored and retrieved with the required metadata fields, even when extraction is partial.
Extraction failures do not crash the evidence workflow or block quarter export.
Storage references are durable and compatible with export generation.
Evidence records can exist without a linked bank transaction.

tasks.title: Implement confirm-first evidence matching suggestions and link persistence
tasks.workstream_goal: Make evidence optional but useful by capturing receipts and matching them to bank transactions with explicit user confirmation.
tasks.purpose: Suggest top candidate matches for evidence items using amount, date, and merchant signals, but require explicit user confirmation before linking.
tasks.input: E1 extracted evidence metadata, B2 transactions, and A1 EvidenceLink contract.
tasks.output: Matching service, ranked top-3 candidate results with reason chips, link confirmation and no-match actions, and EvidenceLink persistence.
tasks.action: Rank candidate transactions conservatively, expose top three matches with human-readable reasons, and persist confirmed links or explicit no-match/later outcomes.
verification: Matching suggestions use amount, date proximity, and merchant similarity signals as required.
No evidence-to-bank link is finalized without an explicit user confirmation action.
Candidate payloads include merchant, date, amount, and match-reason chips.
Confirmed links are available to export generation and remove the related evidence task from relevant queues.

tasks.title: Implement MVP voice intent parser and transaction action dispatcher
tasks.workstream_goal: Enable fast, low-friction voice micro-decisions and reusable merchant rules without expanding beyond MVP intents.
tasks.purpose: Support the voice command set for category, business/personal, split, receipt attach, evidence match selection, and no-match actions on inbox and finish-now flows.
tasks.input: C1 classification handlers, E2 matching actions, supported intent list from the epic, and chosen speech/intent tooling.
tasks.output: Voice intent parser, command-to-action dispatcher, confidence handling, and test fixtures for supported utterances.
tasks.action: Implement the constrained voice command layer and map each recognized intent to the correct existing action handler with safe fallback behavior when confidence is low or context is missing.
verification: Each supported MVP voice command maps to the correct transaction or evidence action.
Intent application is logged or auditable enough to troubleshoot misfires without storing sensitive raw audio unnecessarily.
Unsupported or ambiguous utterances fail safely without corrupting transaction state.
Representative tests cover the minimum 80% voice success target path assumptions.

tasks.title: Add voice confirmation chip and one-tap undo behavior
tasks.workstream_goal: Enable fast, low-friction voice micro-decisions and reusable merchant rules without expanding beyond MVP intents.
tasks.purpose: Provide the required post-voice feedback loop so users can see exactly what was applied and reverse it with a single tap.
tasks.input: F1 dispatched voice actions, C1/C2 mutation history, and UI state requirements for inbox and finish-now views.
tasks.output: Reusable confirmation-chip state model, undo action handler, and UI integration contract for recent voice-applied changes.
tasks.action: Build a reusable feedback model that summarizes the applied voice action and invokes a one-tap rollback against the most recent auditable mutation.
verification: After a successful voice action, the UI can display a single chip summarizing the applied changes.
Undo restores the prior persisted values using the audit/change history rather than ad hoc client state.
Voice-applied changes and manual reversions remain traceable.
The confirmation model works for category, business/personal, split, and evidence-match intents.

tasks.title: Implement merchant rules for always-categorise defaults
tasks.workstream_goal: Enable fast, low-friction voice micro-decisions and reusable merchant rules without expanding beyond MVP intents.
tasks.purpose: Reduce future blocker counts by letting users persist simple merchant-based categorisation defaults within the MVP rule scope.
tasks.input: A1 Rule schema, A2 persistence, C1 classification actions, and merchant normalization patterns from imported transactions.
tasks.output: Rule CRUD/service logic, merchant-pattern matcher, automatic classification application on new transactions, and override behavior.
tasks.action: Implement simple merchant rules that can apply default classifications during import or refresh while preserving the ability for users to override results later.
verification: A saved rule auto-applies to matching future or refreshed transactions within the allowed MVP scope.
User overrides remain possible and are not silently overwritten by later refreshes.
Rule matching is deterministic for common merchant normalization cases.
Rule-driven classifications feed into blocker reduction and audit counts for export reporting.

tasks.title: Build first-time setup and bank connection mobile flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver the MVP mobile screens for onboarding, inbox triage, quarter close, evidence confirmation, and voice-assisted resolution.
tasks.purpose: Implement the user-visible onboarding flow covering sign-up, sole-trader selection, bank connection, disclaimer display, and initial import kickoff.
tasks.input: A3 disclaimer support, B1 bank connection flow, and product journey J1 requirements.
tasks.output: Mobile UI screens for onboarding and connect-bank flow, navigation states, loading/error states, and access/start helper script for local preview.
tasks.action: Create the setup screens and wiring needed for a user to install, sign up, choose sole trader, connect a bank, and trigger the initial 90-day import with clear progress states.
verification: Provide a root-level or relevant-directory access/start script that launches the UI preview and prints the local URL.
Document and verify the localhost URL for the onboarding preview, such as http://localhost:3000/onboarding or the project-standard equivalent.
Run a startup smoke test to confirm the UI boots without immediate dependency or configuration crashes.
Capture and save screenshot evidence of the onboarding flow in a working state.
The flow exposes sole trader as the MVP path and shows the required disclaimer without blocking the connect flow unnecessarily.

tasks.title: Build inbox triage screen with quick actions and blocker-focused resolution
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver the MVP mobile screens for onboarding, inbox triage, quarter close, evidence confirmation, and voice-assisted resolution.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the primary day-to-day inbox experience where users resolve only flagged items through taps or voice-supported quick actions.
tasks.input: B3 inbox read model, C1 classification actions, C2 duplicate actions, and F1/F2 voice integration contracts.
tasks.output: Inbox mobile screen, quick-action components, blocker badges, voice entry affordances, and local preview route/script updates.
tasks.action: Implement the inbox UI so users can review flagged transactions, apply micro-decisions quickly, and see blocker state updates without navigating away from the work queue.
verification: Provide or update an access/start script that launches the inbox UI locally and prints the preview URL.
Document the localhost URL for the inbox screen preview.
Run a startup smoke test covering the inbox screen and confirm it renders with mock or resilient fallback data if dependencies are unavailable.
Capture and save screenshot evidence showing the inbox screen in a working state.
Resolving a blocker from the inbox updates the visible item state and supports the required voice confirmation chip/undo behavior.

tasks.title: Build quarter readiness and Finish Now quarter-close screens
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver the MVP mobile screens for onboarding, inbox triage, quarter close, evidence confirmation, and voice-assisted resolution.
tasks.purpose: Implement the headline MVP quarter-close experience with readiness percentage, items-left messaging, finish-now queue, and export gating.
tasks.input: D1 quarter metrics, D2 finish-now queue, D3 export status rules, and journey J3 requirements.
tasks.output: Quarter screen UI, finish-now queue UI, export CTA states, empty/complete states, and local preview route/script updates.
tasks.action: Create the quarter close screens that show progress, launch the ordered blocking queue, and enable export only when blockers reach zero.
verification: Provide or update an access/start script that launches the quarter-close UI locally and prints the preview URL.
Document the localhost URL for the quarter screen preview.
Run a startup smoke test confirming the quarter and finish-now screens render and handle missing backend dependencies gracefully with fallback data or clear non-fatal messaging.
Capture and save screenshot evidence for the quarter screen and finish-now queue.
A quarter with unresolved blockers shows the correct items-left state, and resolving the final blocker enables export immediately in the UI.

tasks.title: Build evidence match confirmation and attach flow UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Deliver the MVP mobile screens for onboarding, inbox triage, quarter close, evidence confirmation, and voice-assisted resolution.
tasks.purpose: Expose the receipt attach and confirm-first evidence matching experience, including top-3 candidate presentation and no-match/later actions.
tasks.input: E1 evidence capture pipeline, E2 matching suggestions, and journey J4 requirements.
tasks.output: Evidence attach UI, candidate bottom sheet, confirmation actions, no-match/later states, and local preview route/script updates.
tasks.action: Implement the user-facing evidence attach and match-confirmation flow so evidence can be captured, reviewed, and linked only after explicit user choice.
verification: Provide or update an access/start script that launches the evidence UI locally and prints the preview URL.
Document the localhost URL for the evidence matching preview.
Run a startup smoke test confirming the evidence flow renders even when extraction or database-backed matching is temporarily unavailable, using resilient fallback behavior.
Capture and save screenshot evidence of the confirmation bottom sheet in a working state.
The UI shows three candidate slots with merchant, date, amount, and match-reason chips plus No match / Later actions.

tasks.title: Instrument MVP success metrics and event telemetry
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP meets speed, completion, blocker-reduction, and voice-success goals while preserving traceability and regression safety.
tasks.purpose: Measure whether the product can hit the stated MVP goals for quarter-close speed, completion, blocker reduction, and voice success.
tasks.input: Epic success metrics, implemented flows across ingestion, resolution, evidence, voice, and export.
tasks.output: Telemetry/event schema, instrumentation points, KPI aggregation jobs or queries, and metric definitions for dashboards or reports.
tasks.action: Instrument the core user journey events and build the minimal metric calculations needed to evaluate the MVP goals after rollout or during test runs.
verification: Telemetry covers quarter-open to export timing, export completion rate, blocker counts, and voice success rate.
Event names and payloads are consistent with privacy and security constraints.
Metric calculations can be reproduced from captured events without manual spreadsheet work.
Instrumentation does not break core user flows when telemetry sinks are unavailable.

tasks.title: Create end-to-end acceptance suite for quarter close, export, evidence confirmation, and voice undo
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove the MVP meets speed, completion, blocker-reduction, and voice-success goals while preserving traceability and regression safety.
tasks.purpose: Encode the epic acceptance tests and critical regression paths so the MVP can be validated repeatedly without manual guesswork.
tasks.input: Implemented domain, service, export, evidence, voice, and UI flows across workstreams A through G.
tasks.output: Automated end-to-end or integration test suite, fixture datasets, expected export artifacts, and validation documentation/logs.
tasks.action: Build acceptance coverage for the headline quarter-close path, export artifact integrity, evidence confirmation requirements, and undoable voice categorisation behavior using deterministic fixtures.
verification: A fixture quarter with 200 transactions and 8 blockers reproduces the expected items-left behavior.
Resolving the final blockers enables export immediately and generates all 4 artifacts with correct totals and traceability.
Evidence attachment tests require explicit user confirmation or No match before linking.
Voice 'Category: Travel' applies successfully and can be undone with one tap in the covered workflow.
